a_image.alt: Teletext Ceefax style mood board — Broadcast Data · 1974–2012

sections.a_description: Blocky text in cyan, yellow, and white on a pitch-black screen — the unmistakable look of Teletext and Ceefax, the pre-internet information services broadcast alongside television signals from the 1970s to the 2010s. Each page was a grid of fixed-width characters in a handful of pure, saturated colors, delivering news, weather, and TV listings with a visual simplicity that feels almost alien in the age of high-definition screens.

Teletext styling appears on retro broadcasting fan sites, information design retrospectives, and creative coding projects. You'll find it on nostalgia-focused personal pages, alternative social media platforms, and experimental web art — anywhere that celebrates the beauty of information stripped down to its most fundamental, grid-locked visual form.

a_image_cover.alt: Teletext Ceefax style use case illustration
Teletext Ceefax style use case illustration
Teletext Ceefax style use case illustration

sections.a_description: The blocky, low-resolution information display system broadcast alongside analog television — Ceefax (BBC) and Teletext (ITV). Pure black background (#000000). Text rendered in the signature Teletext color palette: white (#ffffff), cyan (#00ffff), yellow (#ffff00), green (#00ff00), magenta (#ff00ff), red (#ff0000), and blue (#0000ff) — all at full saturation, no in-between tones. Characters appear blocky and grid-aligned. The .inner panel has no visible background — just text on black, organized in a rigid grid-like structure. A pseudo-element creates a page header bar: cyan background with black text showing a page number. Everything monospaced. Set in Share Tech Mono — a clean monospace font with slightly technical character that approximates the fixed-width Teletext character grid. Heading at 24px, line-height 1.3, cyan (#00ffff). Eyebrow at 13px, uppercase, 2px letter-spacing, yellow (#ffff00). Body at 15px, line-height 1.6, white. Buttons are blocky rectangles with no border-radius: primary is cyan background, black text. Secondary is yellow text on black with yellow border. Hover swaps to inverted colors. Alternatives: IBM Plex Mono (wider technical feel), Fira Mono (clean monospace), Source Code Pro (neutral).
